text.title: Help Hierarchy and Content Patterns
text.summary: Seven-level help hierarchy, contextual help patterns (inline hints, tooltips, tours), and content type matrix by use case.

section.title: Help Hierarchy
section.p: Prioritized from best to last resort:
1. Interface design that needs no explanation (best — eliminate confusion at source).
2. Inline help — field hints, placeholder text visible during interaction.
3. Tooltips — on-hover explanation for icons and controls.
4. Guided tours — onboarding walkthroughs for new users.
5. FAQs — answers to common questions (5 max per page).
6. Documentation — detailed task-focused articles with numbered steps.
7. Human support (last resort — tickets, chat, email).

rule.statement: Fix the interface before adding help content; help that compensates for a broken design is a band-aid, not a solution.
rule.rationale: Help content that explains a confusing flow treats the symptom; redesigning the flow eliminates the need. Documentation is more expensive to maintain than a clear UI.

section.title: Contextual Help Patterns
section.p: Inline hints (always visible): label + hint text below field, placeholder text in inputs.
Tooltips (on demand): [?] icon hover → brief explanation, [i] icon hover → feature context.
Contextual panels: collapsible sidebar with page-specific help for complex features.
Onboarding tours: step-by-step overlay anchored to specific UI elements with [Skip] and [Next] options.

example.note: Stripe dashboard: contextual tooltip on each metric explains what it measures and how to improve it. Help appears where the user is, not in a separate help center.
Help buried in footer with no contextual help anywhere in the app. Users who get stuck mid-task must navigate away to find answers, losing their context.

section.title: Content Types by Use Case
section.p: Onboarding: welcome tour (first login), empty states (new sections), progress checklist (guide setup). In-context: tooltips (hover), inline hints (always visible), [?] icons (on demand). Reference: FAQs (quick answers), how-to articles (numbered steps), reference docs (complete detail). Support: search (self-service), chat (real-time help), email (complex issues).

rule.statement: Organize help content by user tasks (verbs), not product features (nouns).
rule.rationale: Users search for what they want to accomplish ("how to reset password"), not what the feature is called internally ("Authentication Settings"). Task-based organization matches search intent.
